essence: Analyses CNC machine data to improve performance, reduce downtime, and drive data‑driven manufacturing decisions.

identity.title: CNC Data Analyst
identity.summary: Collects, interprets, and visualises CNC machining data to identify inefficiencies, optimise processes, and support digital manufacturing initiatives.

salary.notes: Analysts in aerospace, defence, or fully digitalised smart‑factory environments can exceed £90k-£120k+.

qualifications: Experience with CNC systems, digital manufacturing, or industrial data
Knowledge of MES, SCADA, IIoT, and machine data capture
Ability to analyse machining performance metrics and trends
Skills in data visualisation, dashboards, and statistical analysis

lifestyle.description: CNC data analysts work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, and Industry 4.0 environments. They turn machine data into actionable insights that improve efficiency, reduce downtime, and support continuous improvement. The role suits individuals who enjoy analytics, optimisation, and digital transformation.

habits: Collects and analyses CNC machine data
Supports digital manufacturing and Industry 4.0 initiatives
Collaborates with engineers, operators, and automation teams
